interpretation: vLLM dense Marlin also falls to the same down-projection weight-cycle band as Ferrum default; m16 down is 69.268us vs Ferrum 68.651us.
Gate_up remains compute-bound and effectively unchanged under weight cycling for both kernels.

The remaining end-to-end gap is not explained by dense Marlin kernel selection. The next bottleneck search should focus on decode integration and scheduling: per-step launch count, non-Marlin work, request batching cadence, and whether vLLM overlaps or batches decode work differently.
